metadata.author: David Ancor
metadata.stakeholders: Founders; Product Managers; Engineering Teams; Team Admins; Compliance Officers; Site Reliability / DevOps
metadata.parent_okr: Ship c1v v1.0 with metric-traceable specs and live feedback loop
metadata.regulatory_refs: SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; PCI-DSS (scope tbd in Module 4)

use_case_name: UC08 — Trace Tech-Stack Recommendation to Metric
initial_conditions: At least one draft tech-stack recommendation has been produced by UC01 or UC06.
The system holds authorized read access to Documentation Sources and Cloud Providers.
The declared metric vocabulary for c1v is fixed as {availability, throughput, latency}.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall receive a draft tech-stack recommendation from the spec generator (UC01) or recommender (UC06) with the technology name and claimed capability.
The system shall parse the recommendation to extract the technology identifier and the claimed performance capability.
The system shall classify the claimed capability into exactly one metric class drawn from {availability, throughput, latency} before querying any external source.
The system shall query Documentation Sources for published performance data matching the technology identifier.

actor_steps_table.other_actors: Documentation Sources return vendor specs, RFCs, and SLA statements matching the technology identifier.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall query Cloud Providers for baseline availability and pricing data matching the technology identifier.

actor_steps_table.extra_actor_col: Cloud Providers return availability zones, regional baselines, and service-tier SLAs matching the technology identifier.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall resolve the recommendation to a concrete value for the classified metric using only the cited external sources.
The system shall validate that the recommendation's claimed capability is supported by at least one cited external source.
The system shall compute a traceback confidence score in the range [0.0, 1.0] for every resolved recommendation.
The system shall record the traceback triple for every recommendation as {recommendation, metric_value, metric_class, source_citation, confidence, cited_at}.
The system shall flag the recommendation for human review if the confidence score is below TRACEBACK_MIN_CONFIDENCE.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Engineering Team Member reviews flagged recommendations and supplies an override or refinement

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall attach the recorded traceback triple to the recommendation in the spec when the confidence score meets or exceeds TRACEBACK_MIN_CONFIDENCE.
The system shall refuse to emit a spec in which fewer than TRACEBACK_COVERAGE_PCT of tech-stack recommendations carry an attached traceback triple.
The system shall record an audit entry for every traceback event with recommendation identifier, metric class, source citation, confidence, and timestamp (CC.R02).
The system shall return the traced recommendation to the calling use case (UC01 or UC06) within TRACEBACK_LATENCY_SEC of receipt.

ending_conditions: Every tech-stack recommendation is linked to a declared performance metric drawn from {availability, throughput, latency} with a cited external source.
Every recommendation carries a recorded traceback confidence score in the range [0.0, 1.0].
Every recommendation whose confidence is below TRACEBACK_MIN_CONFIDENCE is flagged for human review.
No spec is emitted in which fewer than TRACEBACK_COVERAGE_PCT of recommendations carry an attached traceback triple.
An audit trail row exists for every traceback event with recommendation, metric class, source citation, confidence, and timestamp.
notes: Scope anchor: UC08 is the M1 hard-constraint anchor for metric traceability ('Must trace every tech-stack recommendation to a declared performance metric').
Invocation: called as <<include>> by UC01 (on spec assembly) and UC06 (on recommendation generation).
Source constraint: traceback evidence MUST be external (vendor doc, RFC, SLA, cloud provider baseline). Internal benchmarks are a separate provenance class and are not accepted as UC08 citations.
TRACEBACK_COVERAGE_PCT is set to 100 in v1 — no tech-stack recommendation reaches a spec without a traced metric.
TRACEBACK_MIN_CONFIDENCE gates auto-attachment (step 14). Below-threshold recommendations block spec emission (step 15) until a human review resolves them (step 13).
Cross-cutting: audit trail (CC.R02) on every traceback event; cite-source provenance is part of audit row.
Degradation: if Documentation Sources and Cloud Providers are both unreachable, the system shall refuse to attach any traceback and surface the external-source outage to the caller.
Latency: TRACEBACK_LATENCY_SEC is a per-recommendation bound, not a batch bound; UC01 and UC06 budget accordingly.
